title: Fluffy Herbal Tunisian Rice Tagine
description: A light, herbinfused rice dish with galangal and citrus, perfect for a postworkout meal and completely eggfree.
instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then set aside.
Toast the almonds in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2–3 minutes, stirring frequently until fragrant. Remove and set aside.
In a medium pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced galangal, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and smoked paprika; cook for 1 minute until the spices are fragrant.
Add the rinsed rice to the pot and stir to coat the grains with the oil and spices. Cook for 2 minutes, lightly toasting the rice.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18–20 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the pot from heat, fluff the rice gently with a fork, and let it rest for 5 minutes under the lid to finish steaming.
Stir in the lemon zest, lemon juice, and chopped parsley & cilantro, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the fluffy rice to a serving dish, scatter the toasted almonds on top for crunch, and serve warm as a nourishing post‑workout tagine.
